title: Babel
summary: Babel translates modern JavaScript into older JavaScript.
This lets new code run on older browsers.
It works behind the scenes in your build.
It is why you can use the latest features safely.

sections.text: New JavaScript features appear every year, but not every browser understands them right away. Babel solves this. It quietly translates your modern code into an older form that more browsers can run.

sections.title: What Babel Does
sections.text: Babel is a translator for JavaScript. You write using the newest features, and Babel converts that into an older, more widely supported version. The behavior stays the same, but the code becomes something even older browsers can understand. It usually runs as part of your build, unseen.

sections.title: Why It Matters
sections.text: Without Babel, using a brand new feature might break the app for people on older browsers. With it, you write modern, clean code and trust the translation to keep it working everywhere. It is a big reason the ES6 features you learned can be used freely today.

media.alt: Modern JavaScript on one side translated to older JavaScript on the other

sections.title: A Translator at a Meeting
sections.text: Babel is like a translator at a meeting between people who speak different languages. You speak in the modern language you prefer, and the translator delivers it in a language the older audience understands. The message is unchanged, just made understandable to everyone in the room.

media.alt: A translator relaying a modern message into an older language

sections.title: How Babel Fits In
items: You write modern JavaScript with the latest features.
Babel runs as part of your build.
It translates that into an older, supported version.
Older browsers can now run your code.

sections.text: Babel can also understand things that are not plain JavaScript, like the JSX from React, translating it into regular JavaScript calls. It is a quiet helper behind a lot of modern tooling.

terms.definition: A tool that translates modern JavaScript to older.
Converting code from one version to another.
The newest features you write with.
Which browsers can run your code.

questions.explanation: Babel translates modern JavaScript into an older, more supported version.

options: New code can run on older browsers

options: Behind the scenes in your build
